issues.title: Core tracker: CLI + HTML board + skill packaging
issues.description: The v1 build: dependency-free Python CLI, standalone HTML board view, agent skill packaging for Claude Code and Codex.

issues.title: jira.py CLI: init/add/move/comment/set/list/next/show/status/render
issues.description: 629-line stdlib-only CLI; JSON source of truth with auto re-render via save().

issues.title: Standalone HTML board: kanban, by-epic view, filters, detail modal
issues.description: Self-contained board.html with embedded data island; dark theme; no runtime deps beyond optional web fonts.

issues.title: Skill packaging for Claude Code (.claude) and Codex (.codex)
issues.description: SKILL.md workflows 1-4 + operating principles; Codex variant adds agents/openai.yaml.

issues.title: Docs and examples: README, references/schema.md, sample board + rendered HTML
issues.description: Install instructions (global/project), hand-driving guide, schema reference, consistent example board.

issues.title: Hardening: fixes from 2026-06-04 code review
issues.description: Seven confirmed bugs (each reproduced via CLI) plus test coverage and packaging-sync work.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Starting hardening pass: black-box test suite first (RED), then fixes for JT-7..JT-13 (GREEN), then .codex sync + example regeneration.

issues.title: fuzzy() rejects documented status shorthands prog/review
issues.description: SKILL.md:42 and schema.md:50 promise 'prog'/'review' work, but fuzzy() (jira.py:90-100) is prefix-only and 'in progress' does not start with 'prog'. Fix: unique-substring fallback.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Fixed fuzzy() in scripts/jira.py: unique-fragment fallback after prefix matching, so prog/review resolve as documented; ambiguous input now lists its candidates. Tests: test_move_accepts_prog_shorthand / _review_shorthand.

issues.title: list --status <closed> returns nothing without --all
issues.description: Open-only filter at jira.py:236-237 runs after the explicit status filter and wipes Done/Cancelled results. Fix: explicit --status implies --all.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: cmd_list: an explicit --status filter now implies --all, so closed statuses are findable. Test: test_list_status_done_finds_closed_without_all.

issues.title: render mutates board.json (bumps project.updated)
issues.description: cmd_render (jira.py:306-309) goes through save(); a read-only view command rewrites the JSON and causes git diffs. Fix: render_html() directly.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: cmd_render now renders the view directly without save(); board.json is untouched. Test: test_render_does_not_modify_board_json.

issues.title: init accepts unvalidated --key; esc() misses single quotes
issues.description: init --key "A'B</script>" is accepted; keys are interpolated into onclick JS strings (jira.py:484) and esc() (:457) does not escape quotes. Fix: validate ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}$ and escape ' in esc().

comments.author: agent
comments.body: init validates --key against [A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}; template esc() now escapes single quotes. Tests: test_init_rejects_invalid_key, test_template_esc_covers_single_quote. examples/board.html regenerated with the new template.

issues.title: Corrupt board.json shows raw traceback; writes not atomic
issues.description: load() (jira.py:55-60) has no JSONDecodeError handling; save() truncate-writes in place. Fix: friendly die() + temp-file os.replace writes.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: load() dies cleanly on JSONDecodeError; new write_atomic() (temp file + os.replace) used for both board.json and board.html. Tests: test_corrupt_json_dies_cleanly, test_save_leaves_no_temp_files.

issues.title: set --parent allows self-parent and cycles
issues.description: set KEY --parent KEY succeeds (jira.py:200-201). Fix: reject self-parent and walk ancestors to detect cycles.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: New valid_parent() helper rejects self-parenting and ancestry cycles in cmd_set. Tests: test_set_rejects_self_parent, test_set_rejects_parent_cycle.

issues.title: SKILL.md $jira alias pattern breaks under zsh
issues.description: jira="python3 path" then $jira cmd relies on word splitting; zsh (macOS default) does not split. Fix SKILL.md to README-style: jira=<path>; python3 $jira cmd.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: SKILL.md now stores only the script path in the variable and invokes via: python3 $jira (zsh-safe); schema.md wording corrected from unique-prefix to unique fragments. Test: test_skill_md_has_no_zsh_breaking_alias.

issues.title: Add black-box CLI test suite (stdlib unittest)
issues.description: tests/test_jira.py invoking the CLI via subprocess: regression tests for all commands + the seven review bugs. No third-party deps, matching project ethos.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Writing tests/test_jira.py: subprocess-driven CLI tests; failing tests for each review bug + regression coverage for existing behavior.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: tests/test_jira.py added: 26 black-box subprocess tests (14 regression, 10 review-bug, 2 packaging), stdlib unittest only, no deps. Full suite green. Run: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests.

issues.title: Sync mechanism + drift check for .claude/.codex/global installs
issues.description: Installed ~/.claude copy found stale vs repo (missing 4430ba0). Add make sync/install + a test asserting the two in-repo trees are identical.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Half done: test_claude_and_codex_trees_in_sync now enforces byte-identical shared files across both trees. Remaining: make sync/install convenience targets. Global ~/.claude install refreshed manually on 2026-06-04.

issues.title: Add LICENSE file
issues.description: README positions the repo as a shareable template; no license present.

issues.title: Board view enhancements: epic progress rollup, surface In Review items
issues.description: By-Epic view could show x/y done per epic; next/status could call out In Review items as awaiting human review. Also: no CLI command to edit project fields (e.g. repo URL) after init.

issues.title: Always-on board reconciliation after every request in tracked repos
issues.description: In repos with .jira/board.json, every turn ends with an explicit board-reconciliation check once work/thinking completes: update if work state changed, stay silent otherwise. Write model decided 2026-06-04: tiered auto-apply - status moves + comments on actively-worked issues apply immediately and are reported; new issues, retitles, re-priorities still confirmed first.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Brainstorming/design in progress; the new fuzzy 'prog' shorthand works, as it happens.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Design approved (tiered auto-apply + approach B: skill policy + optional UserPromptSubmit hook guarantee layer). Spec committed at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-04-board-reconciliation-design.md, pending user review.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: Shipped: SKILL.md Workflow 5 (end-of-turn reconcile, blocked-write surfacing, red-flags incl. over-track loophole fix), Tiered board writes principle (auto move/comment/render on in-progress or named issues; confirm add/set/seeding/reopens), README five-moments + tiered description + Guarantee layer section, scripts/install-board-hook.py (idempotent, type-guarded, symlink-safe, --global/--settings; 8 tests). Skill-TDD evidence: baseline A dropped a blocked Done-move silently -> GREEN A surfaces exact pending commands; baseline/GREEN B closed the 'too trivial to track' rationalization. Residual noted: one GREEN-B run attempted confirm-tier add directly (caught by permission layer). Suite 34/34; trees byte-identical; global install refreshed.
